title: Blackwater medium
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"22jkb","text":"Blackwater medium is a laboratory growth medium for Hydra that replicates the chemical composition of blackwater swamps. This medium has lower low ionic strength than traditional Hydra medium and contains humic acid. Blackwater medium is ideal for Hydra species isolated from blackwater swamps and improves the health of some transgenic Hydra lines that are prone to infection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Make stock solutions in 50 mL conical tubes with DI water. A B C 1 Component Stock conc. Weight for 50 mL 2 Calcium chloride dihydrate (CaCl2*2H2O) 2.5 M 18.4 g 3 Magnesium chloride hexahydrate (MgCl2*6H2O) 1.0 M 10.2 g 4 Sodium chloride (NaCl) 5.0 M 14.6 g 5 Potassium chloride (KCl) 1.0 M 3.73 g 6 Humic acid sodium salt 20 g/L 1 g Table 1. Stock solutions for blackwater medium.
Not all of the humic acid sodium salt will go into solution. Filter the humic acid solution with a syringe filter or vacuum filter to remove undissolved particles.

Prepare blackwater medium by diluting stock solutions in DI water. Use a 1 L bottle, or scale up volumes for a large plastic carboy. Mix well before using. A B C 1 Component Final conc. Volume of stock for 1 L 2 Calcium chloride dihydrate (CaCl2*2H2O) 0.015 mM 6.0 μL 3 Magnesium chloride hexahydrate (MgCl2*6H2O) 0.0185 mM 18.4 μL 4 Sodium chloride (NaCl) 0.125 mM 25 μL 5 Potassium chloride (KCl) 0.0075 mM 75 μL 6 Humic acid sodium salt 0.01 g/L 0.5 mL Figure 2. Chemical composition of Blackwater medium.
